titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Gerrit Lansing Papers
titlestmt.author: by Beinecke staff

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Monday December 9, 2019 at 06:09

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Granary Books, Inc. on the Ezra Pound Archive Fund, 2011.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Gerrit Lansing Papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Gerrit Lansing Papers. Yale Collection of American Literature, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Gerrit Lansing (1928-2018) was an American poet, editor, and critic. Born in 1928 in Albany, New York, and raised in northern Ohio, he studied at Harvard University and Columbia University. Lansing was a longtime friend and correspondent of poet Charles Olson, with whom he shared an interest in the occult. Lansing was a longtime resident of Gloucester, Massachusetts, where he lived with his partner Deryk Burton and operated occult bookstore Abraxas Books. Lansing edited and published the literary journal Set, and wrote collections of poetry including Heavenly Tree Grows Downward (North Atlantic Books, 1977), Heavenly Tree, Soluble Forest (Talisman House, 1995), and February Sheaf (Pressed Water, 2003).

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections as they are acquired, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.
This collection received a basic level of processing, including rehousing and minimal organization.

Information included in the Description of Papers note and Collection Contents section is drawn from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are often based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Titles have not been verified against the contents of the folders in all cases. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during initial processing.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection and/or revisions in arrangement and description.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into seven series: I. Correspondence, 1945-2002. II. Writings, 1944-1998. III. Other papers, 1956-2002. IV. Photographs, circa 1970s, undated. V. Ken Irby papers, 1964-2008. VI. Stephen Jonas papers, 1958-2001. VII. Set records, 1960-1967.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The collection spans the years 1944-2008 and consists of correspondence, writings, photographs, printed material, and other papers documenting the life and work of poet Gerrit Lansing. Correspondence includes letters from many poets, writers, and other associates, including Deryk Burton, Thomas Meyer, Charles Olson, and Jonathan Williams, as well as members of the Lansing family. Writings include both Lansing's work and writings and broadsides by other poets. The collection includes records relating to the publication of the literary journal Set, as well as writings, correspondence, and other papers relating to poets Kenneth Irby and Stephen Jonas.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into two subseries: Alphabetical correspondence and Family corresondence.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series consists of correspondence to Gerrit Lansing from a wide variety of poets, writers, and others, as well as letters between Lansing and other members of the Lansing family. Literary and personal correspondence is organized alphabetically, and notable correspondents include Deryk Burton, Thomas Meyer, Charles Olson, and Jonathan Williams.

did.unittitle: Arcane, Douglas and Lauren/Draven and Onca

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains correspondence from Aldon L. Nielsen regarding permission to publish poems by Stephan Jonas.

did.unittitle: University Books (Felix Morrow, Kathryn Conroy, and Lyle Stuart)

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into three subseries: Gerrit Lansing wirtings, Writings of others, and Broadsides of others.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series consists of writings by Gerrit Lansing, writings of other poets, and broadsides by other poets. Lansing's wrirings include manuscript and typescript poems, collaborations, college writings, and broadsides.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Published in Hardware Poets Occasional #4, Oct. 1964.

did.unittitle: "By Darklong River: In Praise of Frederick Tuckerman, 1821-1873"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes: "Mummy is Precipitating to Eternity", "Kindness is Shining", "Canto 69", "Twelve was Climbing the Enigma", "A Vertebra will be Painfully Crying for Celebration", "The Doctor was Joyfully Pumping for the Infinitesimal", "Three Plus Four", "Lines Written in Elation at the Passing of Daylight Savings Time", "The Temperamental Phonograph", and "Six No Trump".

College essay - "John Reed as a Romantic"
College essay - "Literature in the High School"
College essay - "Meaning and Interpretation in Literature"
College essay - "The Tales of Howard Phillips Lovecraft"
College writing - "The Abdication of the Heart"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Published in Ashen Meal 1, 1995.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Published in Ashen Meal 2, 1996

"New-England's Rarities Discovered: in Birds, Beasts, Fishes, Serpents, Plants of that Country by John Josselyn, Gent."
"Notes on Nature and Spirit: With Reference to American Spirit"
"A Toast, to Poesy for Robin Blaser"
Harry - A Reflection of a Line in Time

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes an article on Ken Irby and various poems with no known authorship.

did.unittitle: Abel, David. "Near the Mimbres." Salient Seedling Press
Byrd, Don. "Spanda, Time is two, A Meeting with Henry James and Herman Melville on State Street, The Second Coming from My Study Window, etc."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Written for Gerrit Lansing's birthday. Signed and inscribed by the author.

did.unittitle: Driscoll, Karen - "Poems from Journals" - Oasii: Broadside Series no. 2
Eshleman, Clayton - "Colis" - inscribed and signed by the author - Black Sparrow Press Broadside - flyer no. 5
Fisher, Grant - "In the Circle / Galilee," "A solstice greeting from Grant Fisher & Diane di Prima" - printed at Cranium Press in San Francisco
Gallo, Peter - "mishap or stutter, admitted . . ." - Oasii: Broadside Series, no. 5
Irby, Kenneth - "Restless the rain returns . . ." - A Tansy Publication
Joris, Pierre - "Out/Takes" - 232 of 750 copies - inscribed and signed by the author
McClelland, Bruce - "A Pyramid." 3 of 100 copies. Inscribed and signed by the author
Natell, Tom, Charlie Rossiter and Dan Wilcox - "Tree, River, Rock." 2nd edition. 3 Guys from Albany
Parr, Christopher. "At Walden Pond: Late September Inventory"
Pettet, Simon - "Spleen." 100 copies and signed by the author. Kavyayantra Press and The Harry Smith Print Shop
State University of New York at Buffalo, The University Libraries - "Christmas Broadsides"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes material from Joel Oppenheimer (1979), Daphne Marlatt (1980), Alan Bold (1993), Charles Bernstein (1991), Michael Basinski (1992), and Steve McCaffery (1996).

did.unittitle: Roditi, Edouard. "Aphorisms: Life with God the Father." Red Hill Press
Stein, Charles. "Normal Music 2." Open Studio Print Shop
Street, Jason. "From: A Book of Measure." 20 of 50 copies. "Reproduced for christ mass – epiphany"
Strong, Jonathan. "Symphony of Heroes for Gerrit Lansing." Signed by the author
Zimmerman, Daniel. "3 Poems." Oasii: Broadside Series no. 1, 1994.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series consists of flyers, printed material, clippings, juvenalia, and other assorted papers.

did.unittitle: Guy Hocquenghem, "The Homosexual Century: In Search of Gay Memory"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photocopy of interview from the Gay Community News , Feb. 2, NY.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photocopies of articles and CD liner notes.

scopecontent.p: From H. Daniel Peck, ed. The Green American Tradition: Essays and Poems for Sherman Paul , 1989.

Hyde, Lewis - Alcohol and Poetry: John Berryman and the Booze Talking, photocopy

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Business card for the bookstore.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence and an invoice concerning book purchases.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Birth certificate and Probate Court document.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Concerning the buying and selling of two Joseph Cornell boxes.

scopecontent.p: Concerning book donations.

did.unittitle: Grade school and high school memorabilia

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Pages from an unidentified person's loose-leaf notebook.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Flyers, cards and other ephemera of readings and events involving Gerrit Lansing.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series conissts of photographs of Gerrit Lansing, the Lansing family, Charles Olson, and others.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Polaroid photographs of Feidt's paintings. Signed and inscribed by Feidt to Gerrit Lansing.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: One black and white photograph is identified on the back as being by Gerard Malanga.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Kenneth Irby (1936-2015) was an American poet associated with both the Black Mountain School and the L=A=N=G=U=A=G=E group.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: This series is organized into two subseries: Correspondence, and Manuscript material.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series consists of correspondence and writings by or relating to Kenneth Irby and sent to Gerrit Lansing by Irby. The correspondence includes many photocopies of letters written to Irby by others.

did.unittitle: Correspondence copied and sent from Irby to Gerrit Lansing.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Stephen Jonas (1920, 1925, or 1927 to 1970) was a Boston-based African American poet. Gerrit Lansing served as Jonas's literary executor.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series consists of corresondence, writings, and other papers by or relating to poet Stephen Jonas.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into four subseries: Correspondence, Manuscript material, Manuscript photocopies, and general papers.

did.unittitle: Anderson, T.J. to Gerrit Lansing: Originals

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains: "shut the coffin lid…"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains: "Ars Poetica"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains: "A Music Master", and "Song Afta Waller & Others".

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains: "five poems sent to me by G.L.", "the thing", "verbatim ac literatim", and "Three Dance Moods".

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains: "A Little Magic", "i musici", "the outraged genius", and "quie sabe?".

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains: "the orphic voice", "the moon's a bitter grape", and "A Warrant is out for the Arrest of Henry Miller".

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains: "but then what is form…", "A Great Beast", "song", and "to linus who would make his hit quickly".

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains: "XXIV" and "to mcfleknor of no. beach & n.y.", "XXXIII", "XXXIV", "XXXV", "XXXVII", "the can be led but not commissioned to kno" and "Song of Myself", "crux", "down & out theocritan", "Epitaphs", "in nuce", and "O", "a fantasy", "First Matter", "To Flora", "golden apollo", "Haiku XXIII", "unfinished business", "the Bear's Paw", "The Here", "Hermes in the Bible Belt", "not exactly altaforte", "old flame", "plainte de l'oubli des morts", "sort of landscape painting", "thematic exhibitions" versions 1 and 2,"to a disciple", "XXIV", "XXV", and "XXVI", "to iambicus", "tother ways on", and "a tract".

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains: "Blackstone Park","Elusive Cricket", "Enigma", "as to style", "exercise for your ear, no 31", "exercise for your ear, no 30", "post mortem p.s.", "The Process", "the good life", and "microcosmos", and "old refrain".

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains a letter to Gerrit Lansing from Stephen Jonas, including "CXLIX", "CL", "CLI", "CLII", "CLIII", "CLIV", "CLV", "CLVI", "CLVII", "CLVIII", and "CLIX".

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains: "I", "II", "III", "IV", and "nubis in nubibus".

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains: "LXVI"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains: "V" and "VI", "VI", "XII: On the Esplanade", "Against Epitaphs as Such", "for G. Malanga", "Joke", "A Long Poem for Jack Spicer: Books 1-2", "A Long Poem for Jack Spicer: Book 5", two photocopies of "A Long Poem for Jack Spicer: Book 5", "Putdown", "Three Impressions for John Wieners", and "Three Poets in Three Moods".

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains miscellanous poems and correspondence.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Set records are organized into three subseries: Correspondence, Manuscript material, and other papers.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series consists of records relating to the publication of journal Set, of which Gerrit Lansing published two volumes, Set 1 and Set 2, in Winter 1961/62 and Winter 1963/64, respectively.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Mold remediation was performed on box 13 in Series VII.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Visible mold was present upon receipt in Box 13, but is no longer active. Use caution when handling.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains: "The Calgary Eye-Opener" (not published) and "A Bedroom High" (not published).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains: "Some, Man, On the Street".

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains: "The Suicide", and "Where Fled".
